Control Objective: Documented network diagram and data flows
Remediation Action: Create new network and data-flow diagrams

Control Objective: Firewall rules restrict inbound/outbound traffic to business need
Current State: Draft complete; secondary verification identified a configuration gap. While the network diagram is drafted; an audit of the live environment revealed an unrestricted inbound rule on Port 22 for [Asset ID].
Gap Description: Inbound firewall rules for [Asset Name/ID] are configured to allow unrestricted access to Port 22 (SSH) from the public internet (0.0.0.0/0). This contradicts the internal Network Security Policy. Inbound firewall rules for [Asset Name/ID] are configured to allow unrestricted access to Port 22 (SSH) from the public internet (0.0.0.0/0). This contradicts the internal Network Security Policy; which requires all remote access to be restricted to authorized IP ranges or via a VPN.
Remediation Action: Restrict inbound SSH (Port 22) traffic to specific; authorized IP addresses (e.g.; Company VPN or Office Static IP) and disable 0.0.0.0/0 access

Current State: Administrative Gap Identified. The live cloud console identifies the database subnet as DB-PROD-VPC; but the official Network Diagram labels it as CDE-Database-Zone
Gap Description: Naming convention inconsistency between technical environment and official compliance documentation
Remediation Action: Synchronize labels in the Network Diagram to match the live AWS Cloud Console naming

Control Objective: Change management for firewall rule changes
Current State: Unauthorized Change Detected: Port 22 opened on March 15 without a corresponding Jira ticket.
Gap Description: Breach of formal Change Management policy; high-risk modification performed without approval.
Remediation Action: 1. Revert change. 2. Root-cause analysis. 3. Restrict IAM permissions.

Current State: Audit of 15 Windows Jump Servers revealed the "Guest" account is still enabled on 4 legacy nodes.
Gap Description: Information leakage risk: Default OS "Guest" account is active; allowing unauthorized read-access to directory structures.
Remediation Action: Disable the "Guest" account via Group Policy (GPO) and verify across all CDE assets.

Control Objective: Default passwords changed before connecting to network
Current State: Default password changed, but new password 'Admin123' fails complexity requirements.
Gap Description: Non-compliance with Password Policy: Device was updated but uses a weak password that lacks required length and special characters.
Remediation Action: Update device credentials to meet the 14-character minimum and complexity standards; re-verify.

Current State: New Linux server (SRV-PAY-09) deployed with default 'Full' install, including active Telnet and FTP services.
Gap Description: Critical hardening gap: Insecure legacy protocols (Telnet, FTP) are active; transmitting credentials in plain-text over the CDE network.
Remediation Action: 1. Disable Telnet/FTP services immediately. 2. Apply CIS Level 1 Hardening Baseline. 3. Re-scan to verify closure.

Control Objective: PAN rendered unreadable (tokenization, truncation, hashing or encryption)
Current State: Internal Support Portal displays full 16-digit PAN to all 'Tier 1' support agents.
Gap Description: Violation of 'Need to Know' principle: Full credit card numbers are visible to unauthorized staff instead of being truncated to the last 4 digits.
Remediation Action: Modify the UI code to implement masking (e.g. ************1234) for all non-finance roles.

Current State: Documented SOP exists for key generation and rotation, but lacks a specific section for 'Key Destruction'.
Gap Description: Minor Documentation Gap: The existing Key Management policy does not explicitly define the process for securely deleting retired cryptographic keys.
Remediation Action: Update the Key Management SOP to include a 'Retirement and Destruction' section; formally approve the new version.

Control Objective: Retention and data minimization policies enforced
No insecure protocols (SSL/early TLS) for CHD
Malware protection on all applicable systems including servers
Monitoring and alerting integrated with SIEM
Secure SDLC with code review and SAST/DAST
MFA for administrative and remote access
Current State: Company VPN (Cisco AnyConnect) is configured for Username/Password only; MFA is not enforced for remote employees.
Gap Description: Critical Security Gap: Remote access to the corporate network lacks a second factor of authentication; violating PCI DSS Requirement 8.3.
Remediation Action: Enable Duo/Okta MFA integration on the VPN Gateway and enforce for all user profiles.

Control Objective: Log retention (12 months) and daily reviews
Annual penetration tests and after significant changes
Security policy reviewed at least annually
Third-party risk management for service providers
